meta.title: GEONYX ARMOR | Mineral Industrial Floor System
meta.description: GEONYX ARMOR – superflat mineral floor system engineered for extreme mechanical loads, VNA aisles, racking zones and heavy industry halls.

hero.description: MINERAL ARMOR FOR HEAVY INDUSTRY, MINING AND ENERGY. Composite system for tracked vehicles, abrasion and impact loads with Design Life ≥20 years under industrial operation.

diagnostics.title: INDUSTRIAL AGGRESSION. WE TAKE IT.
diagnostics.subtitle: 8 critical mechanical and operational loads that standard concrete was not designed for.

d1.title: EXTREME ABRASION
d1.problem: Friction from tracks, tyres and ore acts like sandpaper on concrete, removing millimetres of material every year.
d1.solution: Mineral surface with Mohs 9 hardness and AR ≤ 0.5 – minimal volume loss after 1000 abrasion cycles.

d2.title: CONCENTRATED LOAD
d2.problem: Tracks, heavy axles and crane wheels concentrate tonnes of load onto a small contact area, splitting the surface.
d2.solution: Compressive strength >110 N/mm² and high shear resistance – a monolithic layer that does not delaminate or crush under tracked vehicles.

d3.title: IMPACT LOAD
d3.problem: Falling parts, buckets, dies and tools crack the concrete, break edges and open 'nests' for further damage.
d3.solution: Ductile mineral matrix that absorbs impact energy without sudden brittle fracture – edges remain intact and the surface stays stable.

d4.title: VIBRATION
d4.problem: Constant vibrations from turbines, presses and cranes cause material fatigue, micro-cracks and coating delamination.
d4.solution: Monolithic bond with the substrate and vibration-resistant structure – the system acts as a single body, without voids or weak zones.

d5.title: JOINTS AND BROKEN EDGES
d5.problem: Joints and edges are the weakest zone on industrial floors. Impacts from forklifts and hard wheels break edges, form pits and create vibrations in equipment.
d5.solution: GEONYX ARMOR enables monolithic or minimal-joint solutions with reinforced edges. Where joints exist, they are structurally protected so they do not fail under heavy vehicle traffic.

d6.title: DYNAMIC FATIGUE LOAD
d6.problem: Constant traffic along the same routes causes material fatigue. Micro-cracks, local subsidence and 'ruts' appear in high-traffic zones.
d6.solution: The mineral ARMOR system distributes dynamic loads in depth and prevents the formation of fatigue cracks, ensuring a stable surface even under 24/7 operation.

d7.title: LOCAL MACHINE FOUNDATIONS
d7.problem: Heavy machinery and anchored structures concentrate loads on small support points. This leads to crushing and cracking around anchors with standard concrete.
d7.solution: GEONYX ARMOR builds a high-strength surface with excellent compressive and shear resistance, suitable for integration with machine foundations without local failure.

d8.title: PRODUCTION DOWNTIME
d8.problem: Every repair of joints and pits stops production, blocks equipment and often costs more than the material itself.
d8.solution: ARMOR systems with REPAIR protocols – local repairs within 24–48 hours and phased work without prolonged line stoppages.

anatomy.sectionSubtitle: Interactive layer model – from the substrate to the working surface.

h1.title: 1. CONCRETE SUBSTRATE
h1.subtitle: Load-bearing structure
h1.desc: Concrete substrate – load-bearing structure (existing concrete, steel or old pavement).

h2.title: 2. PREPARATION AND ADHESION LAYER
h2.subtitle: CSP Profile
h2.desc: Mechanical profiling (CSP profile) and mineral bond with the substrate.

h3.title: 3. STRUCTURAL COMPOSITE LAYER
h3.subtitle: Reinforcement
h3.desc: Reinforced mineral matrix 8–12 mm (up to 25 mm under extreme load).

h4.title: 4. SURFACE PROTECTION LAYER
h4.subtitle: Texture
h4.desc: Finely tuned texture for grip and easy cleaning.

engineering.title: EVIDENCE FROM THE LABORATORY
engineering.subtitle: Independent tests in accredited laboratories – full protocols available in TDS.
engineering.block1note: GEONYX ARMOR approaches diamond hardness and outperforms standard concrete and steel in wear resistance.
engineering.block2note: Up to 8 times less volume loss after 1000 friction cycles compared to ordinary industrial concrete.
engineering.block3note: Linear performance without sudden fracture – composite matrix that absorbs energy instead of breaking.

r1.title: MOISTURE TOLERANCE
r1.desc: Application on 'green' concrete and substrates with residual moisture. Breathable structure that reduces the risk of osmotic pressure and delamination.

r2.title: SPARK-FREE
r2.desc: ATEX logic for mines, fuel and ammunition depots. The surface does not generate mechanical sparks on impact.

r3.title: CLASS XF4 / FREEZE–THAW
r3.desc: Full immunity to freeze/thaw cycles and salts (de-icing agents). Suitable for external ramps, quays and hangars.

r4.title: JOINTLESS OR MINIMAL-JOINT SYSTEM
r4.desc: Zone-based design with a minimum number of industrial joints – fewer repair points and lower risk of failure.

r5.title: GLOBAL CERTIFICATION
r5.desc: EN 13813, CE Mark, ISO 9001. Full engineering documentation for public and private investors.

financial.title: WHEN THE FLOOR IS AN INVESTMENT, NOT A COST
financial.subtitle: Total cost of ownership comparison over 20 years – standard concrete with periodic repairs versus GEONYX ARMOR with minimal interventions.
financial.quote: CONCRETE GETS PAID FOR MANY TIMES OVER.

roi.title: INVESTMENT AUDIT
roi.subtitle: 20-year operational cycle comparison between standard concrete and GEONYX ARMOR under 3-shift industrial operation.

applications.title: WHERE IT IS USED
applications.subtitle: Facilities with high mechanical aggression, heavy traffic and extreme loads.

a1.title: Heavy Metallurgy
a1.desc: Foundries, rolling mills, slag and hot-metal zones.

a2.title: Logistics Centres
a2.desc: VNA aisles, ramps, loading docks and intensive forklift traffic.

a3.title: Mines and Processing Plants
a3.desc: Crushers, mills, flotation areas and heavy-equipment service bays.

a4.title: Energy
a4.desc: Turbine halls, maintenance platforms, coal and ash bunkers.

a5.title: Automotive Manufacturing
a5.desc: Press shops, assembly lines, welding robots and test zones.

a6.title: Defence Industry
a6.desc: Repair bases for tracked vehicles, ammunition depots (ATEX).

a7.title: Recycling and Waste
a7.desc: Scrap yards, sorting installations and waste disposal sites.

a8.title: Aviation
a8.desc: Maintenance hangars, heavy undercarriage zones and engine service areas.

process.title: EXECUTION PROCESS
process.subtitle: Engineering control from the first analysis to final site acceptance.

p1.title: Engineering Audit
p1.desc: Load analysis, zoning and risk profile.

p2.title: Design
p2.desc: Determination of thickness (8–25 mm) and system.

p3.title: Substrate Preparation
p3.desc: Mechanical profiling (CSP profile), cleaning, moisture control.

p4.title: Application
p4.desc: Application of the ARMOR composite system according to the technical protocol.

p5.title: Quality Control
p5.desc: Verification of adhesion, thickness and levelness.

p6.title: Documentation
p6.desc: Documented handover with technical data sheet.

offer.qualityDesc: All documents comply with current Eurocodes and standards.

cta.title: DESIGN FOR 20 YEARS. NOT FOR 3.
cta.subtitle: Engineering system for facilities with extreme mechanical loads and high operational risk.
cta.desc: Full technical documentation and engineering consultation for investors, designers and operators in heavy industry and critical infrastructure.

footerBand.label: TECHNOLOGY PARTNER FOR CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE
footerBand.desc: You need a system that can withstand mines, steelworks, logistics hubs or energy facilities. GEONYX ARMOR combines geopolymer technology, laboratory data and field testing to deliver a floor that can be defended before any investor and auditor.
